text: AI is increasingly common in the workplace, bringing both opportunities and challenges. Many routine tasks are now handled through automation, which can quickly process data or manage schedules. This frees up employees to focus on more complex and creative work. Productivity tools powered by AI, such as smart assistants and project management software, help teams stay organized and meet deadlines. However, the rise of AI also means some jobs will change or disappear, requiring workers to learn new skills. The most effective workplaces are those that encourage human-AI collaboration, where people and machines work together, combining human judgment with AI's speed and accuracy.
